chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: This is a 3. It's sloppily written and rendered at an extremely low resolution of 28 by 28 pixels,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: but your brain has no trouble recognizing it as a 3. And I want you to take a moment to appreciate

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: how crazy it is that brains can do this so effortlessly. I mean this, this, and this are

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: also recognizable as 3s, even though the specific values of each pixel is very different from one

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: image to the next. The particular light-sensitive cells in your eye that are firing when you

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: see this 3 are very different from the ones firing when you see this 3. But something

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: in that crazy smart visual cortex of yours resolves these as representing the same idea, while

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: at the same time recognizing other images as their own distinct ideas. But if I told you,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: hey, sit down and write for me a program that takes in a grid of 28 by 28 pixels like this

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and outputs a single number between 0 and 10, telling you what it thinks the digit is,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: well the task goes from comically trivial to dauntingly difficult. Unless you've been

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: living under a rock, I think I hardly need to motivate the relevance and importance of

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: machine learning and neural networks to the present and to the future, but what I want

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: to do here is show you what a neural network actually is, assuming no background, and to help

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: visualize what it's doing. Not as a buzzword, but as a piece of math. My hope is just that you

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: come away feeling like the structure itself is motivated, and to feel like you know what

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: it means when you read or hear about a neural network quote unquote learning. This video is

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: just going to be devoted to the structure component of that, and the following one is going to tackle

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: learning. What we're going to do is put together a neural network that can learn to recognize handwritten digits.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: This is a somewhat classic example for introducing the topic, and I'm happy to stick with the status quo here,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: because at the end of the two videos I want to point you to a couple good resources where you

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: can learn more, and where you can download the code that does this and play with it on your own computer.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: There are many, many variants of neural networks, and in recent years there's been sort of a boom

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: in research towards these variants, but in these two introductory videos you and I are just going to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: look at the simplest plain vanilla form with no added frills. This is kind of a necessary prerequisite

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: for understanding any of the more powerful modern variants, and trust me it still has plenty of

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: complexity for us to wrap our minds around. But even in this simplest form it can learn to recognize

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: handwritten digits, which is a pretty cool thing for a computer to be able to do. And at the same time

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: you'll see how it does fall short of a couple hopes that we might have for it. As the name suggests, neural

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: networks are inspired by the brain, but let's break that down. What are the neurons and in what sense

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: are they linked together? Right now when I say neuron, all I want you to think about is a thing that holds

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: a number, specifically a number between 0 and 1. It's really not more than that. For example, the network

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: starts with a bunch of neurons corresponding to each of the 28x28 pixels of the input image, which is

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: 784 neurons in total. Each one of these holds a number that represents the grayscale value of the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: corresponding pixel, ranging from 0 for black pixels up to 1 for white pixels. This number inside the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: neuron is called its activation, and the image you might have in mind here is that each neuron is lit up

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: when its activation is a high number. So all of these 784 neurons make up the first layer of our network.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Now jumping over to the last layer, this has 10 neurons, each representing one of the digits.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: The activation in these neurons, again some number that's between 0 and 1, represents how much the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: system thinks that a given image corresponds with a given digit. There's also a couple layers in between,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: called the hidden layers, which for the time being should just be a giant question mark for how on earth

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: this process of recognizing digits is going to be handled. In this network I chose two hidden layers,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: each one with 16 neurons, and admittedly that's kind of an arbitrary choice. To be honest, I chose two

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: layers based on how I want to motivate the structure in just a moment, and 16? Well that was just a nice

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: number to fit on the screen. In practice there is a lot of room for experiment with a specific structure

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: here. The way the network operates, activations in one layer determine the activations of the next layer.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And of course the heart of the network, as an information processing mechanism, comes down to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: exactly how those activations from one layer bring about activations in the next layer.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: It's meant to be loosely analogous to how in biological networks of neurons, some groups of neurons firing

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: cause certain others to fire. Now the network I'm showing here has already been trained to recognize

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: digits. Let me show you what I mean by that. It means if you feed in an image, lighting up all 784

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: neurons of the input layer according to the brightness of each pixel in the image, that pattern of

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: activations causes some very specific pattern in the next layer, which causes some pattern in the one

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: after it, which finally gives some pattern in the output layer. And the brightest neuron of that output

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: layer is the network's choice, so to speak, for what digit this image represents.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And before jumping into the math for how one layer influences the next, or how training works,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: let's just talk about why it's even reasonable to expect a layered structure like this to behave

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: intelligently. What are we expecting here? What is the best hope for what those middle layers might be doing?

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Well, when you or I recognize digits, we piece together various components. A 9 has a loop up top,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and a line on the right. An 8 also has a loop up top, but it's paired with another loop down low.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: A 4 basically breaks down into 3 specific lines, and things like that. Now in a perfect world,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: we might hope that each neuron in the second to last layer corresponds with one of these subcomponents.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: That any time you feed in an image with, say, a loop up top, like a 9 or an 8, there's some specific

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: neuron whose activation is going to be close to 1. And I don't mean this specific loop of pixels,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: the hope would be that any generally loopy pattern towards the top sets off this neuron. That way,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: going from the third layer to the last one just requires learning which combination of subcomponents

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: corresponds to which digits. Of course, that just kicks the problem down the road, because how would you

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: recognize these subcomponents, or even learn what the right subcomponents should be? And I still

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: haven't even talked about how one layer influences the next, but run with me on this one for a moment.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Recognizing a loop can also break down into subproblems. One reasonable way to do this would

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: be to first recognize the various little edges that make it up. Similarly, a long line, like the kind

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: you might see in the digits 1, or 4, or 7, well that's really just a long edge. Or maybe you think of it as a

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: certain pattern of several smaller edges. So maybe our hope is that each neuron in the second layer

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: of the network corresponds with the various relevant little edges. Maybe when an image like this one

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: comes in, it lights up all of the neurons associated with around 8 to 10 specific little edges, which in

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: turn lights up the neurons associated with the upper loop and a long vertical line, and those light up the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: neuron associated with a 9. Whether or not this is what our final network actually does is another

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: question, one that I'll come back to once we see how to train the network. But this is a hope that we

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: might have, a sort of goal with the layered structure like this. Moreover, you can imagine how being able

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: to detect edges and patterns like this would be really useful for other image recognition tasks.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And even beyond image recognition, there are all sorts of intelligent things you might want to do

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: that break down into layers of abstraction. Parsing speech, for example, involves taking raw audio and

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: picking out distinct sounds, which combine to make certain syllables, which combine to form words,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: which combine to make up phrases and more abstract thoughts, etc. But getting back to how any of this

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: actually works, picture yourself right now designing how exactly the activations in one layer might

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: determine the activations in the next. The goal is to have some mechanism that could conceivably combine

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: pixels into edges, or edges into patterns, or patterns into digits. And to zoom in on one very specific

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: example, let's say the hope is for one particular neuron in the second layer to pick up on whether or

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: not the image has an edge in this region here. The question at hand is what parameters should the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: network have? What dials and knobs should you be able to tweak so that it's expressive enough to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: potentially capture this pattern, or any other pixel pattern, or the pattern that several edges can

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: make a loop, and other such things? Well, what we'll do is assign a weight to each one of the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: connections between our neuron and the neurons from the first layer. These weights are just numbers.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Then take all of those activations from the first layer and compute their weighted sum according to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: these weights. I find it helpful to think of these weights as being organized into a little grid of their own,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and I'm going to use green pixels to indicate positive weights and red pixels to indicate negative weights,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: where the brightness of that pixel is some loose depiction of the weights value.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Now if we made the weights associated with almost all of the pixels zero, except for some positive weights in this region that we care about,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: then taking the weighted sum of all the pixel values really just amounts to adding up the values of the pixel just in the region that we care about.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And if you really wanted to pick up on whether there's an edge here,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: what you might do is have some negative weights associated with the surrounding pixels.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Then the sum is largest when those middle pixels are bright, but the surrounding pixels are darker.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: When you compute a weighted sum like this, you might come out with any number,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: but for this network, what we want is for activations to be some value between zero and one.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: So a common thing to do is to pump this weighted sum

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: into some function that squishes the real number line into the range between zero and one.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And a common function that does this is called the sigmoid function, also known as a logistic curve.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Basically, very negative inputs end up close to zero,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: very positive inputs end up close to one, and it just steadily increases around the input zero.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: So the activation of the neuron here is basically a measure of how positive the relevant weighted sum is.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: But maybe it's not that you want the neuron to light up when the weighted sum is bigger than zero.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Maybe you only want it to be active when the sum is bigger than, say, 10.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: That is, you want some bias for it to be inactive.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: What we'll do then is just add in some other number, like negative 10, to this weighted sum

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: before plugging it through the sigmoid squishification function.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: That additional number is called the bias.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: So the weights tell you what pixel pattern this neuron in the second layer is picking up on,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and the bias tells you how high the weighted sum needs to be

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: before the neuron starts getting meaningfully active.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And that is just one neuron. Every other neuron in this layer is going to be connected to all

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: 784 pixel neurons from the first layer, and each one of those 784 connections has its own weight

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: associated with it. Also, each one has some bias, some other number that you add on to the weighted

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: sum before squishing it with the sigmoid. And that's a lot to think about! With this hidden layer of 16

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: neurons, that's a total of 784 times 16 weights, along with 16 biases. And all of that is just the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: connections from the first layer to the second. The connections between the other layers also have

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: a bunch of weights and biases associated with them. All said and done, this network has almost exactly

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: 13,000 total weights and biases. 13,000 knobs and dials that can be tweaked and turned to make this

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: network behave in different ways. So when we talk about learning, what that's referring to is getting

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: the computer to find a valid setting for all of these many many numbers so that it'll actually

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: solve the problem at hand. One thought experiment that is at once fun and kind of horrifying is to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: imagine sitting down and setting all of these weights and biases by hand, purposefully tweaking the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: numbers so that the second layer picks up on edges, the third layer picks up on patterns, etc.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: I personally find this satisfying rather than just treating the network as a total black box,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: because when the network doesn't perform the way you anticipate, if you've built up a little bit of

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: a relationship with what those weights and biases actually mean, you have a starting place for

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: experimenting with how to change the structure to improve. Or when the network does work but not

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: for the reasons you might expect, digging into what the weights and biases are doing is a good way to

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: challenge your assumptions and really expose the full space of possible solutions. By the way,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: the actual function here is a little cumbersome to write down, don't you think?

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: So let me show you a more notationally compact way that these connections are represented.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: This is how you'd see it if you choose to read up more about neural networks.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: See this, the matrix. Ariamente, all, let me draw down, and we see you now on a rail arm in

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: the sameonut edit as a argot. S Sandleton Consortium Democratic values,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: of course, in the same way, I root on theCINGS will continue the things that I choose, and I

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: mean that we are wasting all of this novaium in eachvector feels like this normal return,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: of the terms in the matrix vector product of everything we have on the left here.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: By the way, so much of machine learning just comes down to having a good grasp of linear algebra,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: so for any of you who want a nice visual understanding for matrices and what matrix

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: vector multiplication means, take a look at the series I did on linear algebra, especially chapter

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: 3. Back to our expression, instead of talking about adding the bias to each one of these values

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: independently, we represent it by organizing all those biases into a vector, and adding the entire

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: vector to the previous matrix vector product. Then as a final step, I'll wrap a sigmoid around the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: outside here, and what that's supposed to represent is that you're going to apply the sigmoid function

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: to each specific component of the resulting vector inside. So once you write down this weight matrix

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and these vectors as their own symbols, you can communicate the full transition of activations

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: from one layer to the next in an extremely tight and neat little expression. And this makes the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: relevant code both a lot simpler and a lot faster, since many libraries optimize the heck out of matrix

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: multiplication. Remember how earlier I said these neurons are simply things that hold numbers? Well,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: of course, the specific numbers that they hold depends on the image you feed in.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: So it's actually more accurate to think of each neuron as a function, one that takes in the

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: outputs of all the neurons in the previous layer, and spits out a number between 0 and 1.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Really, the entire network is just a function, one that takes in 784 numbers as an input,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: and spits out 10 numbers as an output. It's an absurdly complicated function, one that involves

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: 13,000 parameters in the forms of these weights and biases that pick up on certain patterns, and which

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: involves iterating many matrix vector products and the sigmoid squishification function, but it's just a function nonetheless.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And in a way, it's kind of reassuring that it looks complicated. I mean, if it were any simpler,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: what hope would we have that it could take on the challenge of recognizing digits?

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And how does it take on that challenge? How does this network learn the appropriate weights and biases

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: just by looking at data? Well, that's what I'll show in the next video, and I'll also dig a little more into

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: what this particular network we're seeing is really doing. Now is the point I suppose I should say

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: subscribe to stay notified about when that video or any new videos come out, but realistically,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: most of you don't actually receive notifications from YouTube, do you? Maybe more honestly,

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: I should say subscribe so that the neural networks that underlie YouTube's recommendation algorithm

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: are primed to believe that you want to see content from this channel get recommended to you.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Anyway, stay posted for more. Thank you very much to everyone supporting these videos on Patreon.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: I've been a little slow to progress in the probability series this summer, but I'm jumping

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: back into it after this project, so patrons, you can look out for updates there.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: To close things off here, I have with me Leisha Lee, who did her PhD work on the theoretical side of

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: deep learning, and who currently works at a venture capital firm called Amplify Partners, who kindly

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: provided some of the funding for this video. So Leisha, one thing I think we should quickly bring up is

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: this sigmoid function. As I understand it, early networks used this to squish the relevant weighted

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: sum into that interval between zero and one, you know, kind of motivated by this biological analogy

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: of neurons either being inactive or active. Exactly. But relatively few modern networks actually use

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: sigmoid anymore. Yeah. It's kind of old school, right? Yeah, or rather, RELU seems to be much easier to train.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: And RELU stands for Rectified Linear Unit? Yes. It's this kind of function where you're just taking

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: a max of zero and A, where A is given by what you were explaining in the video. And what this was

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: sort of motivated from, I think, was partially by a biological analogy with how neurons would either

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: be activated or not. And so if it passes a certain threshold, it would be the identity function. But if it

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: did not, then it would just not be activated. So it'd be zero. So it's kind of a simplification.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: Using sigmoids didn't help training, or it was very difficult to train at some point. And people

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: just tried RELU. And it happened to work very well for these incredibly deep neural networks.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning
chunks.text: All right. Thank you, Leisha.

chunks.title: But what is a neural network Deep learning

text: This is a 3. It's sloppily written and rendered at an extremely low resolution of 28 by 28 pixels, but your brain has no trouble recognizing it as a 3. What are the neurons and in what sense are they linked together? Right now when I say neuron, all I want you to think about is a thing that holds a number, specifically a number between 0 and 1. It's really not more than that. For example, the network starts with a bunch of neurons corresponding to each of the 28x28 pixels of the input image, which is 784 neurons in total. Each one of these holds a number that represents the grayscale value of the corresponding pixel, ranging from 0 for black pixels up to 1 for white pixels. This number inside the neuron is called its activation, and the image you might have in mind here is that each neuron is lit up when its activation is a high number. So all of these 784 neurons make up the first layer of our network. Now jumping over to the last layer, this has 10 neurons, each representing one of the digits. The activation in these neurons, again some number that's between 0 and 1, represents how much the system thinks that a given image corresponds with a given digit. There's also a couple layers in between, called the hidden layers, which for the time being should just be a giant question mark for how on earth this process of recognizing digits is going to be handled. In this network I chose two hidden layers, each one with 16 neurons, and admittedly that's kind of an arbitrary choice. In practice there is a lot of room for experiment with a specific structure here. The way the network operates, activations in one layer determine the activations of the next layer. And of course the heart of the network, as an information processing mechanism, comes down to exactly how those activations from one layer bring about activations in the next layer. It's meant to be loosely analogous to how in biological networks of neurons, some groups of neurons firing cause certain others to fire. Now the network I'm showing here has already been trained to recognize digits. Let me show you what I mean by that. It means if you feed in an image, lighting up all 784 neurons of the input layer according to the brightness of each pixel in the image, that pattern of activations causes some very specific pattern in the next layer, which causes some pattern in the one after it, which finally gives some pattern in the output layer. And the brightest neuron of that output layer is the network's choice, so to speak, for what digit this image represents. And before jumping into the math for how one layer influences the next, or how training works, let's just talk about why it's even reasonable to expect a layered structure like this to behave intelligently. Now in a perfect world, we might hope that each neuron in the second to last layer corresponds with one of these subcomponents. And I don't mean this specific loop of pixels, the hope would be that any generally loopy pattern towards the top sets off this neuron. The goal is to have some mechanism that could conceivably combine pixels into edges, or edges into patterns, or patterns into digits. The question at hand is what parameters should the network have? What dials and knobs should you be able to tweak so that it's expressive enough to potentially capture this pattern, or any other pixel pattern, or the pattern that several edges can make a loop, and other such things? Well, what we'll do is assign a weight to each one of the connections between our neuron and the neurons from the first layer. These weights are just numbers. Then take all of those activations from the first layer and compute their weighted sum according to these weights. I find it helpful to think of these weights as being organized into a little grid of their own, and I'm going to use green pixels to indicate positive weights and red pixels to indicate negative weights, where the brightness of that pixel is some loose depiction of the weights value. Now if we made the weights associated with almost all of the pixels zero, except for some positive weights in this region that we care about, then taking the weighted sum of all the pixel values really just amounts to adding up the values of the pixel just in the region that we care about. And if you really wanted to pick up on whether there's an edge here, what you might do is have some negative weights associated with the surrounding pixels. Then the sum is largest when those middle pixels are bright, but the surrounding pixels are darker. When you compute a weighted sum like this, you might come out with any number, but for this network, what we want is for activations to be some value between zero and one. So a common thing to do is to pump this weighted sum into some function that squishes the real number line into the range between zero and one. And a common function that does this is called the sigmoid function, also known as a logistic curve. Basically, very negative inputs end up close to zero, very positive inputs end up close to one, and it just steadily increases around the input zero. So the activation of the neuron here is basically a measure of how positive the relevant weighted sum is. But maybe it's not that you want the neuron to light up when the weighted sum is bigger than zero. Maybe you only want it to be active when the sum is bigger than, say, 10. That is, you want some bias for it to be inactive. What we'll do then is just add in some other number, like negative 10, to this weighted sum before plugging it through the sigmoid squishification function. That additional number is called the bias. So the weights tell you what pixel pattern this neuron in the second layer is picking up on, and the bias tells you how high the weighted sum needs to be before the neuron starts getting meaningfully active. The connections between the other layers also have a bunch of weights and biases associated with them.
